flashcards.front: What is the primary goal of CI/CD in implementation?
flashcards.back: To automate code integration and deployment while ensuring continuous testing.

flashcards.front: What distinguishes black-box from white-box testing?
flashcards.back: Black-box tests external behavior without code access; white-box examines internal structures.

flashcards.front: Name two key metrics for test coverage.

flashcards.back: Re-testing existing features after changes to ensure no new defects.

flashcards.front: Which testing level validates module interactions?

flashcards.back: Framework containing test drivers/stubs to execute automated tests.
Test design technique dividing input data into valid/invalid classes.

flashcards.front: Why are boundary values important in testing?
flashcards.back: Most errors occur at extreme ends of input ranges.

flashcards.front: What's the purpose of smoke testing?
flashcards.back: Quick verification of critical functionalities before deep testing.
Introducing artificial defects to evaluate test suite effectiveness.
Test-Driven Development: Write tests before implementation.
